zotero.abstractNote: We present an open-source, modular, torque-controlled robot architecture designed for legged locomotion research. The architecture is based on a custom-designed actuator module that combines a high-torque brushless DC motor with a low-ratio planetary gear and a custom-designed motor controller. The modularity of the design allows for the rapid prototyping of different robot configurations, from simple 1-DOF test benches to complex quadrupedal robots. We demonstrate the capabilities of the architecture by building a quadrupedal robot and showing its performance in various locomotion tasks, including walking, trotting, and jumping. The entire design, including the mechanical parts, electronics, and software, is open-source and available to the research community.
